lede: A look at May 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Gukesh Dommaraju and Issac Luke, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tara Air Flight 197
events.desc: Tara Air Flight 197 crashes in Nepal's Mustang District, killing 22.

events.title: Norilsk oil spill
events.desc: An oil spill in Norilsk releases 17,500 tons of diesel oil into nearby rivers.

events.title: Olusegun Obasanjo
events.desc: Olusegun Obasanjo takes office as President of Nigeria, the first elected and civilian head of state in Nigeria after 16 years of military rule.

events.title: Edmund Hillary
events.desc: Edmund Hillary and Sherpa Tenzing Norgay become the first people to reach the summit of Mount Everest, on Tenzing Norgay's (adopted) 39th birthday.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I veterans begin to assemble in Washington, D.C., in the Bonus Army to request cash bonuses promised to them to be paid in 1945.

events.title: 1920 Louth flood
events.desc: The Louth flood of 1920, a severe flash flood in the Lincolnshire market town of Louth, results in 23 fatalities in 20 minutes. It has been described as one of the most significant flood disasters in the United Kingdom during the 20th century.

events.title: N'Djamena
events.desc: N'Djamena is founded as Fort-Lamy by the French commander Émile Gentil.

events.title: Sojourner Truth
events.desc: Sojourner Truth delivers her famous Ain't I a Woman? speech at the Woman's Rights Convention in Akron, Ohio.

events.title: Fall of Constantinople
events.desc: Fall of Constantinople: Ottoman armies under Sultan Mehmed II capture Constantinople after a 53-day siege, ending the Roman Empire after over 2,000 years.

events.title: Mongol conquest of the Jin dynasty
events.desc: Mongol–Jin war: The Mongols enter Kaifeng after a successful siege and begin looting in the fallen capital of the Jin dynasty.

births.name: Pierre Bourque (politician)
births.line: Canadian businessman and politician, 40th Mayor of Montreal

births.name: Abdoulaye Wade
births.line: Senegalese academic and politician, 3rd President of Senegal

births.name: Harry Kadwell
births.line: Australian rugby league player and coach (died 1999)

deaths.text: Bernard Kerik, American police officer, 40th Police Commissioner of New York City and interior minister of the Iraqi Coalition Provisional Authority
Andrew Greeley, American priest, sociologist, and author
W. S. Gilbert, English playwright and poet
Franciszek Mirecki, Polish composer, music conductor, and music teacher

observances.text: Ascension of Bahá'u'lláh (Baháʼí Faith) (Only if Baháʼí Naw-Rúz falls on March 21 of the Gregorian calendar)
Christian feast day: Pope Alexander of Alexandria (Eastern Orthodox Church)
